tool.name: Chopper
tool.description: Filtering and trimming of long reads.
tool.command:
chopper --input $input --threads "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}"
#if $contam --contam $contam #end if
#if $option_params.quality --quality $option_params.quality.value #end if
#if $option_params.maxqual --maxqual $option_params.maxqual.value #end if
#if $option_params.minlength --minlength $option_params.minlength.value #end if
#if $option_params.maxlength --maxlength $option_params.maxlength.value #end if
#if $option_params.mingc --mingc $option_params.mingc.value #end if
#if $option_params.maxgc --maxgc $option_params.maxgc.value #end if
##trimming #set $ta = $trim_approach.trim_approach #if str($ta) != "" --trim-approach $ta #if $ta == "fixed-crop" --headcrop ${str($trim_approach.headcrop)} --tailcrop ${str($trim_approach.tailcrop)} #else if $ta == "trim-by-quality" --cutoff ${str($trim_approach.cutoff)} #else if $ta == "best-read-segment" --cutoff ${str($trim_approach.cutoff)} #else if $ta == "split-by-low-quality" --cutoff ${str($trim_approach.cutoff)} --split-window ${str($trim_approach.split_window)} #end if #end if
##output capture #if $output_params.inverse == "yes" $output_params.inverse #end if
#if str($input.ext).endswith("gz") | gzip > $fq_filt #else > $fq_filt #end if

param.label: Reference FASTA
param.help: FASTA file with reference to check potential contaminants against.

param.label: Minimal quality score
param.help: Sets a minimum Phred average quality score.

param.label: Maximal quality score
param.help: Sets a maximum Phred average quality score.

param.label: Sets a minimum read length
param.help: Minimal length of read to keep.

param.label: Sets a maximum read length
param.help: Maximal length of read to keep

param.label: Minimum GC content
param.help: Sets a minimum GC content for reads to keep.

param.label: Maximum GC content
param.help: Sets a maximum GC content for reads to keep.

param.label: Trimming approach
param.help: Trimming is applied after quality/length filtering. Leave as 'No trimming' to disable.
param.option: Fixed crop (remove a fixed number of bases from read ends)
Trim by quality (trim low-quality bases from both ends)
Best read segment (keep the highest-quality segment of each read)
Split by low quality (split reads at low-quality stretches)

param.label: Head crop
param.help: Trim this many nucleotides from the start of each read.

param.label: Tail crop
param.help: Trim this many nucleotides from the end of each read.

param.label: Quality cutoff (Q-score)
param.help: Bases below this Phred quality at the read ends are trimmed until a base meets the cutoff.

param.label: Quality cutoff (Q-score)
param.help: Minimum per-base Phred quality used to extract the highest-quality segment of each read.

param.label: Quality cutoff (Q-score)
param.help: Stretches of bases below this Phred quality are treated as low-quality split points.

param.label: Split window
param.help: Minimum number of consecutive sub-cutoff bases required to split a read.

param.label: Output the opposite of the normal results
param.help: Reverse the output results (aka, the 'failed reads')

tool.help:
**Chopper**
Rust implementation of NanoFilt+NanoLyse, both originally written in Python. This tool, intended for long read sequencing such as PacBio or ONT, filters and trims a fastq file. Filtering is done on average read quality, minimal or maximal read length, and GC content, while printing the reads passing the filter.
**Trimming**
Trimming is opt-in via the *Trimming approach* selector and is applied after the filtering steps. Choose one of:
- **Fixed crop** (``--trim-approach fixed-crop``): remove a fixed number of bases from the start (``--headcrop``) and/or end (``--tailcrop``) of each read. - **Trim by quality** (``--trim-approach trim-by-quality``): trim low-quality bases from both ends until a base with quality ≥ the ``--cutoff`` Q-score is reached. - **Best read segment** (``--trim-approach best-read-segment``): keep only the highest-quality segment of each read, based on ``--cutoff``. - **Split by low quality** (``--trim-approach split-by-low-quality``): split reads at low-quality stretches and output the high-quality parts. A read is only split once a run of consecutive sub-``--cutoff`` bases reaches the ``--split-window`` length (default 1); shorter dips are tolerated and kept inside the segment, while leading and trailing low-quality bases are still trimmed.
Compared to the Python implementation the scope is to deliver the same results, almost the same functionality, at much faster execution times. At the moment this tool does not support filtering using a sequencing_summary file.
**More Information**
- **Official Repository**: `Chopper on GitHub`_
.. _Chopper on GitHub: https://github.com/wdecoster/chopper
